description: A giant woody grass with hollow stems; the fastest growing plant, strong yet flexible. 'Pholui' names what bends without breaking—strength through yielding.
sound_symbolism: The word bends rising. 'Pho' /ɸo/ breathes with hollow flexibility, stem bending. 'Lu' rises with rapid growth. Then 'i' springs back upright.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo pholui' (the bamboo). Grass, not tree. For building, crafts, food, paper.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Bamboo as teacher—bending with wind, yielding without breaking, the wisdom of flexibility.
pillars.solarpunk-values: Fastest renewable—bamboo regrows quickly, sequesters carbon, replaces slow-growth timber.
